test_cases.input_summary: Investigating the funding sources of a think tank that publishes research opposing climate regulation, registered as a 501(c)(3) in the US
test_cases.notes: Excellent use of Form 990, foundation grant databases, and indirect tracing strategies. Conflict-of-interest checklist was sharply targeted to the energy policy context. Opacity indicators were well-explained.

test_cases.input_summary: Tracing the funding of a grassroots campaign group opposing a local development project, registered as a UK charity
test_cases.notes: Good adaptation to UK Charity Commission context. Correctly identified the difference between charity accounts and company filings. Indirect tracing via property records and local government submissions was creative.

test_cases.input_summary: Investigating whether a patient advocacy group is funded by pharmaceutical companies, for a healthcare reporting story
test_cases.notes: Pharma transparency databases (Dollars for Docs, Open Payments) were correctly identified. Conflict-of-interest checklist was well-calibrated to the healthcare context. Could have been slightly more concise.
